_comment: END-OF-DAY (EOD) BATCH EXAMPLE. The classic batch pattern: load small dimension feeds (fx rates, client limits) ONCE, then replay the day's trades through the DAG as a finite stream. A BatchingSubscriptionNode coalesces many trades into one envelope; RowCalculatorBatchAdapter applies the row-wise ReferenceEnrichCalculator across the batch; FlatteningPublicationNode unbatches to one enriched message per trade on the way out. Uses the canonical perftest trade stream (now carrying client_id) so EOD client-exposure enrichment works on the same data as the other examples. Runs with no Kafka. Feed it with: python -m perftest.run_eod_example
name: perftest_eod_batch
description: trade stream (mem, batched) -> reference enrichment (fx + client limits) -> flatten -> mem sink. Standalone form of the 'eod_batched' pipeline in run_eod_example.py.

config._note: Replays the day's trades (perftest.generate_trades.make_trade). A bounded file is just a finite stream.
One enriched message per trade (price_usd, notional_usd, client exposure / limit breach).
The adapter lets a plain row calculator process a whole batch envelope; the wrapped calculator is unchanged from its single-row use.
